title: Approved Bloatware or Adware Removal
description: Remove one exact unwanted app after identifying install ownership, startup/persistence links, approval, uninstall, verification, and handoff report.
starter_prompt: Remove this approved unwanted, bloatware, or adware application. Identify it first, uninstall only the exact approved target, preserve user data unless explicitly approved, verify removal, and report leftovers.

required_inputs.label: Exact approved app or package name

phases.prompt: Confirm the exact approved target '${app_name}'. If the name is broad or ambiguous, ask the user for the exact product/package before removing anything.
phases.completion: Exact removal target is clear or human input is requested.

arguments.command: $ErrorActionPreference='Continue'; $name='${app_name}'; function Section($n){ Write-Output ('== ' + $n + ' ==') }; Section 'Registry uninstall matches'; Get-ItemProperty HKLM:\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Uninstall\*,HKLM:\Software\WOW6432Node\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Uninstall\*,HKCU:\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Uninstall\* -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Where-Object { $_.DisplayName -like "*$name*" } | Select-Object DisplayName,DisplayVersion,Publisher,InstallLocation,UninstallString,QuietUninstallString,PSChildName | Format-List; Section 'Winget matches'; winget list --disable-interactivity --accept-source-agreements 2>$null | Select-String -Pattern $name -SimpleMatch; Section 'Appx matches'; Get-AppxPackage -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Where-Object { $_.Name -like "*$name*" -or $_.PackageFullName -like "*$name*" } | Select-Object Name,Publisher,Version,PackageFullName | Format-List; Section 'Services matches'; Get-Service | Where-Object { $_.Name -like "*$name*" -or $_.DisplayName -like "*$name*" } | Select-Object Name,DisplayName,Status,StartType | Format-Table -AutoSize; Section 'Scheduled task matches'; Get-ScheduledTask -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Where-Object { $_.TaskName -like "*$name*" -or $_.TaskPath -like "*$name*" } | Select-Object TaskPath,TaskName,State | Format-Table -AutoSize; Section 'Startup command matches'; Get-CimInstance Win32_StartupCommand -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Where-Object { $_.Name -like "*$name*" -or $_.Command -like "*$name*" } | Select-Object Name,Command,Location,User | Format-List; Section 'Proxy and browser policy clues'; netsh winhttp show proxy; reg query HKCU\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Edge /s 2>$null; reg query HKCU\Software\Policies\Google\Chrome /s 2>$null

phases.prompt: Collect read-only exact-target evidence across uninstall keys, package manager, Appx packages, services, scheduled tasks, startup, proxy, and browser policy clues.
phases.completion: Target identity, ownership, and possible persistence links are captured.

phases.prompt: Analyze target evidence for '${app_name}'. Decide whether the app identity is exact enough to uninstall, identify package/native uninstall route, and list leftovers that must not be deleted without separate approval.
phases.completion: Uninstall route and safety notes are documented.

phases.prompt: Ask human or apply access-mode policy before uninstalling '${app_name}'. Offer restore point when needed. Do not remove user data or browser profiles.
Use SAK package manager first to uninstall the exact approved target '${app_name}'. Capture package selection and uninstall result.

arguments.command: $ErrorActionPreference='Continue'; $name='${app_name}'; Write-Output '== Remaining uninstall entries =='; Get-ItemProperty HKLM:\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Uninstall\*,HKLM:\Software\WOW6432Node\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Uninstall\*,HKCU:\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Uninstall\* -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Where-Object { $_.DisplayName -like "*$name*" } | Select-Object DisplayName,DisplayVersion,Publisher,InstallLocation | Format-List; Write-Output '== Remaining Appx matches =='; Get-AppxPackage -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Where-Object { $_.Name -like "*$name*" -or $_.PackageFullName -like "*$name*" } | Select-Object Name,Version,PackageFullName | Format-List; Write-Output '== Remaining services/tasks/startup matches =='; Get-Service | Where-Object { $_.Name -like "*$name*" -or $_.DisplayName -like "*$name*" } | Select-Object Name,DisplayName,Status,StartType | Format-Table -AutoSize; Get-ScheduledTask -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Where-Object { $_.TaskName -like "*$name*" -or $_.TaskPath -like "*$name*" } | Select-Object TaskPath,TaskName,State | Format-Table -AutoSize; Get-CimInstance Win32_StartupCommand -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Where-Object { $_.Name -like "*$name*" -or $_.Command -like "*$name*" } | Select-Object Name,Command,Location,User | Format-List

phases.prompt: Verify whether the approved target remains in installed apps, Appx packages, services, scheduled tasks, or startup commands.
phases.completion: Removal is verified or leftovers are captured.

phases.prompt: Review post-uninstall evidence. List leftovers, risk, and exact cleanup commands that would require separate approval. Do not claim leftovers were removed unless evidence proves it.

Create a technician-readable bloatware/adware removal handoff report with target identity, evidence, actions/tools used, uninstall method, result, verification, artifacts/logs, leftover items, user-data preservation, cleanup status, residual risk, and next steps.
phases.completion: User has a clear removal handoff report.

phases.prompt: Preserve uninstall and verification evidence; remove only temporary scratch files created by this workflow.

acceptance_criteria: Exact target identity is collected before removal.
SAK package manager is tried before raw uninstall commands.
Uninstall is approval-gated and restore point is offered when needed.
User data, browser profiles, and unrelated apps are preserved.
Verification checks installed state and persistence leftovers.
Report separates removed target from leftovers requiring separate approval.
